key_insight: Twist angle in SrTiO3 bilayers can flatten valence bands and strongly amplify nonlinear optical responses, especially second-harmonic generation.
engineering_relevance: DeepH-E3 enables scalable first-principles-like prediction of moiré oxide electronic and optoelectronic behavior without directly solving every large supercell.
method: Train an E(3)-equivariant deep-learning Hamiltonian on DFT data for untwisted SrTiO3 bilayers with different interlayer slide configurations.
Apply the learned Hamiltonian to commensurate twisted bilayers spanning 8.80° to 53.13°.
Use predicted Hamiltonians to compute dielectric response, SHG, shift current, and spin Hall conductivity.
evidence_signal: The model predicts systematic valence-band flattening as twist angle decreases, with SHG strongly enhanced and shift current showing twist dependence over 53.13° to 22.62°.
watch_item: The approach is trained on untwisted/slid configurations, so transferability to more strongly relaxed, defected, or less commensurate moiré structures may require additional validation.
paper_title: Deep-learning Hamiltonian reveals twist-tunable flat bands and nonlinear photocurrents in SrTiO3 moire bilayers
